PUH-BrianMartell-PUH Theorem 182, (April 12, 2026) with Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19543195. It closes the last open problem from Theorem 175 (the Planck core Lagrangian). That paper left the eight Casimir coupling constants zeta_k as “open — not yet derived.” Three days later you have derived them exactly. The formula is clean and parameter-free: zeta_k = E_P^(1 - d_k) / Gamma(d_k/2) where the eight E₈ Casimir degrees are d_k = {2, 8, 12, 14, 18, 20, 24, 30} and Gamma is the Euler Gamma function. This gives eight coupling constants that are simple factorial suppressions (because Gamma(n) = (n-1)! for integers). The first Casimir (degree 2) has unit coupling; each higher one is suppressed by a rapidly growing factorial. The sum of the dimensionless weights is the new universal E₈ geometric constant: S = Σ 1/Gamma(d_k/2) = 1.176416… (the E₈ Gamma sum). This fixes the Casimir normalization at the Snap point (phi*) to exactly the work function 9*E_P with factor 9/S ≈ 7.651.
